title: josephia hewardiana
side_effects: Josephia hewardiana can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems, particularly when ingested or applied topically in excess. Ingestion of large quantities can lead to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps. Prolonged exposure to its essential oils can cause headaches and dizziness in some individuals. Furthermore, improper use of the plant may trigger anaphylaxis in rare cases.
